claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 12 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you please tell me how many children you're looking to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and wanted to check if I'm eligible.

I have two children - Alex who is 12, and Blake who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's great to meet you, and lovely to hear about Alex (12) and Blake (8).

Now, let me ask you a few more questions to assess your eligibility.

Are both Alex and Blake currently living with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they both live at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you.

Are you responsible for both children? By this I mean, do you pay for things like their food, clothes, and other day-to-day needs?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for both of them - I pay for everything they need like food, clothes, school things, all of that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Wonderful, thank you for confirming that.

Now, could you tell me about your residency status? Are you currently living in the UK, and do you have the right to live here (for example, are you a British citizen, have settled status, or another immigration status that gives you the right to live in the UK)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester actually. And yes, I have the right to live here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you for that information.

Just a couple more questions:

Is either Alex or Blake currently in hospital, residential care, or local authority care, or have they been at any point recently?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them are in hospital or care or anything like that. They're both just at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

One more question: Are both Alex and Blake either in full-time education or approved training, or are they under 16 years old?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, they're both under 16 - Alex is 12 and Blake is 8, so they're both still in primary and secondary school.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Let me just review the eligibility rules to make sure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for all that information. Based on what you've told me and the eligibility rules, I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! Based on the information you've provided, **you appear to be eligible for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake**. Here's a summary:

### Alex (age 12) - ✅ Eligible
- Alex is under 16 years old
- Alex lives with you
- You are responsible for Alex and pay for their day-to-day needs
- You live in the UK with the right to reside
- Alex is not in hospital or care

### Blake (age 8) - ✅ Eligible
- Blake is under 16 years old
- Blake lives with you
- You are responsible for Blake and pay for their day-to-day needs
- You live in the UK with the right to reside
- Blake is not in hospital or care

You meet the main qualifying criteria: you live in the UK, have the right to reside here, and you're responsible for children under 16 who live with you.

If you'd like to make a claim, you can do so through the official government channels. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 12 years old, which is under 16. Alex lives with the claimant, who is responsible for them and pays for their day-to-day needs (food, clothes, school things). The claimant lives in the UK and has the right to reside here. Alex is not in hospital or care. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.

Blake is 8 years old, which is under 16. Blake lives with the claimant, who is responsible for them and pays for their day-to-day needs (food, clothes, school things). The claimant lives in the UK and has the right to reside here. Blake is not in hospital or care. All eligibility criteria are met for Blake.

The claimant pays for all their day-to-day needs including food, clothes, and school items. Both children are under 16 and neither is in hospital or care. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for both children.
